name: Andre Kostelanetz
abstract: Andre Kostelanetz (Russian: Абрам Наумович Костелянец; December 22, 1901 – January 13, 1980) was a Russian-American popular orchestral music conductor and arranger who was one of the major exponents of popular orchestra music.

concerts.label: Ferde Grofé and His Symphony Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: Andre Kostelanetz, Percy Faith, those were typical of the upper end of things you might say... I was one, could really do any number of jobs that were called on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: And, of course, you know that Claude at the time was managing me. And he had access to the studio. In fact, he was arranging for Andre Kostelanetz at the time.

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: They had to be songs that were sung by people like Frank Sinatra, Doris Day, and instrumentalists like Percy Faith and André Kostelanetz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: You know Andre Kostelanetz was famous. Everything, he was so particular with intonation.
about_quotes.doc_title: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Norwich, New York, November 14, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: which incidentally came after my experience having done things for Andre Kostelanetz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
